segments.text: Abraham by now was old, really old, and the Lord had blessed him in every possible way.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: At that time Abraham told his oldest servant who was in charge of his whole household,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Put your hand under my thigh,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: and swear an oath by the Lord, the God of heaven and the God of earth, that you won't arrange for my son to marry any daughter of the these Canaanite people that I'm living among.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Instead, go to my homeland where my relatives live, and find a wife there for my son Isaac.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: What if the woman refuses to come back with me to this country?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: the servant asked.
Should I take your son back to the country you came from?
segments.speaker: The servant

segments.text: No, you mustn't take my son back there,
segments.speaker: Sarah

segments.text: Abraham replied.

The Lord, the God of heaven, took me from my family home and my own country. He spoke to me and swore an oath to me in which he promised, ‘I will give this land to your descendants.’ He is the one who will send his angel ahead of you so that you can find a wife there for my son.
segments.speaker: The LORD God

segments.text: However, if the woman refuses to return here with you, then you are released from this oath. But make sure you don't take my son back there.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: The servant put his hand under the thigh of his master Abraham and swore an oath to do as he had been told.
Then the servant arranged for ten of his master's camels to carry all kinds of valuable gifts from Abraham and left for the town of Nahor in Aram-naharaim.
segments.speaker: Sarah

segments.text: Arriving in the evening, he had the camels kneel down by the spring that was outside the town. This was the time when women went out to fetch water.
segments.speaker: the Angel of the Lord

segments.text: He prayed,
segments.speaker: Abraham's Servant

segments.text: Lord, the God of my master Abraham, please let me be successful today, and please show your faithfulness to my master Abraham.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Look, I'm standing here beside this spring, and the young women of the town are coming to get water.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: May it happen like this. The young woman that I ask, ‘Please hold your water jar so I can have a drink,’ and she replies, ‘Please drink, and I'll give your camels water too’ —may she be the one you've chosen as a wife for your servant Isaac. This way I'll know that you've shown your faithfulness to my master.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: He hadn't even finished praying when he saw Rebekah coming to get water, carrying her water jar on her shoulder. She was the daughter of Bethuel, son of Milkah. Milkah was the wife of Abraham's brother Nahor.
She was very beautiful, a virgin—no one had slept with her. She went down to the spring, filled her jar, and came back up.
segments.speaker: Hagar

segments.text: The servant ran over to meet her and asked,
segments.speaker: the Angel of God

segments.text: Please let me drink a few sips of water from your jar.
segments.speaker: the Angel of God

segments.text: Please drink, my lord,
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: she replied. She quickly lifted the jar down from her shoulder and held it for him to drink.
After she finished giving him a drink, she said,
Let me get water for your camels too until they've had enough.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: She quickly emptied her jar into the trough and ran back to the spring to get more water. She brought enough for all his camels.
The man observed her in silence to see if the Lord had made his journey successful or not.
Once the camels had finished drinking, he gave her a gold nose-ring and two heavy gold bracelets for her wrists.
segments.speaker: Abimelech

segments.text: Then he asked her,
segments.speaker: Abimelech

segments.text: Whose daughter are you? Also could you tell me, is there room in your father's house for us to spend the night?
segments.speaker: The servant

segments.text: She replied,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: I'm the daughter of Bethuel, the son of Milcah and Nahor.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Then she added,
We have plenty of straw and food for the camels,
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: and yes, we have room for you to spend the night.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: The man kneeled down and bowed in worship to the Lord.
segments.speaker: Abimelech

segments.text: Thank you Lord, the God of my master Abraham,
segments.speaker: Abraham's Servant

segments.text: he prayed.
You have not forgotten your commitment and faithfulness to my master. And Lord, you have led me directly to the home of my master's relatives!
segments.speaker: The servant

segments.text: She ran to her mother's house and told her family what had happened.
Rebekah had a brother named Laban, and he ran out to meet the man who had remained at the spring.
segments.speaker: Abimelech

segments.text: He'd noticed the nose-ring and the bracelets she was wearing, and he'd heard his sister Rebekah explaining,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: This is what the man told me.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: When he arrived the man was still there, standing with his camels beside the spring.
Please come home with me, you who are blessed by the Lord,
segments.speaker: Abimelech

segments.text: said Laban.
segments.speaker: Abimelech

segments.text: What are you standing out here for? I've got a room at home ready for you, and a place for the camels to stay.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: So the man went home with him. Laban unloaded the camels and gave them straw and food to eat. He also provided water for the man to wash his feet, as well as for the men who were with him.
Then Laban had food brought in. But the man told him,
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: I'm not going to eat until I've explained why I'm here.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Please explain,
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Laban replied.
I'm Abraham's servant,
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: the man began.
The Lord has blessed my master so much, and now he is a wealthy and powerful man. The Lord has given him sheep and cattle, silver and gold, male and female servants, and camels and donkeys.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: His wife Sarah has had a son for my master even in her old age, and my master has given him everything he owns.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: My master made me swear an oath, saying, ‘You must not arrange for my son to marry any daughter of the Canaanite people in whose land I'm living.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Instead, go to my family home where my relatives live, and find a wife there for my son Isaac.’
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: I said to my master, ‘What if the woman refuses to come back with me?’
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: He told me, ‘The Lord, in whose presence I have lived my life, will send his angel with you, and he will make your journey successful—you will find a wife for my son from my relatives, from my father's family.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: You will be released from the oath you swear to me if, when you go to my family, they refuse to let her return with you.’
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Today when I arrived at the spring, I prayed, Lord, God of my master Abraham, please let the journey I have taken be successful.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Look, I'm standing here beside this spring. May it happen like this. If a young woman comes to get water, and I say, ‘Please give me a few sips of water to drink,’
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: and she says to me, ‘Please drink, and I'll get water for your camels too’ —may she be the one you've chosen as a wife for your servant Isaac.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: I hadn't even finished praying silently when I saw Rebekah coming to get water, carrying her water jar on her shoulder. She went down to the spring to get water, and I said to her, ‘Please give me a drink.’
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: She quickly lifted the jar down from her shoulder and she said, ‘Please drink, and I'll get water for your camels too.’ So I drank, and she got water for the camels.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: I asked her, ‘Whose daughter are you?’ She replied, ‘I'm the daughter of Bethuel, the son of Milcah and Nahor.’ So I put the ring in her nose, and the bracelets on her wrists.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Then I kneeled down and bowed in worship to the Lord. I thanked the Lord, the God of my master Abraham, for he led me directly to find my master's niece for his son.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: So please tell me now, will you show commitment and faithfulness to my master? Please tell me yes or no so I can decide what to do next.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Laban and Bethuel replied,
segments.speaker: Laban and Bethuel

segments.text: Clearly all this is from the Lord, so we can't argue one way or the other.
segments.speaker: Laban and Bethuel

segments.text: Rebekah's here, you can take her and leave. She can become the wife of your master's son, as the Lord has decided.
segments.speaker: Laban and Bethuel

segments.text: As soon as Abraham's servant heard their decision, he bowed down in worship to the Lord.
Then he unpacked silver and gold jewelry and expensive clothes and gave them to Rebekah. He also gave valuable presents to her brother and her mother.
He and the men with him ate and drank, and spent the night there. When they got up in the morning, he said,
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Let me leave now and go home to my master.
segments.speaker: Laban and Bethuel

segments.text: But her brother and her mother said,
segments.speaker: Laban and Bethuel

segments.text: Let her stay with us for another ten days or so. She can leave after that.
segments.speaker: Laban and Bethuel

segments.text: Please don't delay me,
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: he told them.
The Lord has made my journey successful, so let me leave and go back to my master.
segments.speaker: The servant

segments.text: Let's call Rebekah and find out what she wants to do,
segments.speaker: Laban and Bethuel

segments.text: they suggested.
They called Rebekah in and asked her,
Do you want to go with this man now?
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Yes, I'll go,
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: she replied.
So they let Laban's sister Rebekah leave with Abraham's servant and his men, together with the woman who had nursed her as a child.
They asked a blessing on her, saying,
segments.speaker: Rebekah's Family

segments.text: Our dear sister, may you become the mother to thousands and thousands of descendants, and may they conquer their enemies.
segments.speaker: Laban and Bethuel

segments.text: Then Rebekah and her servant girls got on the camels. They followed Abraham's servant and left.
Meanwhile Isaac, who was living in the Negev, had just come back from Beer-lahai-roi.
He went out into the fields one evening to think things over.  He looked into the distance and saw camels coming.
Rebekah was also keeping a look out. When she saw Isaac, she got down from her camel.
She asked the servant,
Who is this walking through the fields to meet us?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He's my master, Isaac,
segments.speaker: he

segments.text: he replied. So she put on her veil to cover herself.
The servant told Isaac everything he'd done.
Isaac took Rebekah into his mother Sarah's tent, and he married her. He loved her, and she brought him comfort after his grief over his mother's death.
